findings.comment: Invoke-ContinuousCoReviewBoundedVerification is never called by the reviewer/orchestrator path, and no declared-command set is transported into Invoke-ContinuousCoReviewReviewer. The prompt nevertheless claims every reviewer test run is executed with this timeout/output/hash wrapper, while the spawned reviewer can invoke shell commands directly. The new tests call the helper itself, so they prove only a fixture-owned substitute, not the changed production behavior. Wire declared commands and wrapper results through the real review invocation (or revise the contract to an enforceable host boundary) and add an end-to-end test proving an agent-requested verification cannot bypass containment.

The bounded runner uses ReadToEndAsync for both streams and truncates only after the child exits, so output is accumulated without bound in memory; a noisy or hostile declared command can exhaust the reviewer process before MaxOutputBytes is applied. The limit is also enforced with string Length/Substring although the parameter and contract are bytes, so multibyte output can exceed the promised cap. Stream into a genuinely byte-bounded buffer while concurrently draining/discarding overflow, and test sustained/multibyte output through the production boundary.
